title: Tenant schema and context with name, slug, email, settings, and status

story.i_want_to: implement a Tenant schema with name, slug, email, settings (jsonb), and status enum, along with a Tenants context module providing CRUD operations
story.so_that: the system has an organizational unit for multi-tenant isolation and every downstream entity can be scoped to a tenant

rationale: Tenants are the root of the multi-tenancy hierarchy. Every API key, project, agent, and piece of data belongs to a tenant. The tenant schema must exist before any other tenant-scoped entity can be created. The settings jsonb field provides tenant-level configuration (rate limits, max projects, etc.) that governs system behavior per-tenant without schema migrations.

acceptance_criteria.description: A Loopctl.Tenants.Tenant schema exists with fields: id (binary_id), name (string), slug (string), email (string), settings (map/jsonb), status (enum: active, suspended, deactivated), inserted_at, updated_at
The Tenant schema does NOT use tenant_id (it is the root entity, not tenant-scoped) — uses Loopctl.Schema with tenant_scoped: false
The slug field is unique, validated as URL-safe (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens), between 2-63 characters
The email field is validated with a basic email format check
The settings field defaults to an empty map %{} and is validated as a map (not a list or scalar)
The status field defaults to :active on creation
A Loopctl.Tenants context module provides: create_tenant/1, get_tenant/1, get_tenant_by_slug/1, update_tenant/2, list_tenants/0 (for superadmin), and suspend_tenant/1, activate_tenant/1
A get_tenant_settings/2 function returns a specific setting value with fallback to a default when the key is not set in the tenant's settings
The tenants table has a unique index on slug and an index on status
The tenants table is NOT subject to RLS policies (it has no tenant_id column)

test_cases.name: Create tenant with valid attributes
expected_results: Tenant has a binary UUID id

test_cases.name: Reject duplicate slug
expected_results: Returns {:error, %Ecto.Changeset{}} with error on slug: "has already been taken"

test_cases.name: Reject invalid slug format
steps: Attempt to create a tenant with slug "INVALID SLUG!"
Attempt to create a tenant with slug "a" (too short)
Attempt to create a tenant with slug containing spaces
expected_results: All attempts return {:error, %Ecto.Changeset{}} with validation error on slug

test_cases.name: Reject invalid email format
expected_results: Returns {:error, %Ecto.Changeset{}} with validation error on email

test_cases.name: Update tenant settings
expected_results: Settings now contain the rate_limit_requests_per_minute key with value 500

technical_notes: Tenant status enum: Ecto.Enum with values [:active, :suspended, :deactivated]
Slug validation regex: ~r/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$/ (starts and ends with alphanumeric)
Settings field should use :map type in Ecto schema, stored as jsonb in PostgreSQL
Default settings values from PRD section 5.3: max_concurrent_agents: 10, max_projects: 50, etc.
The tenants table should NOT have RLS enabled since it is the root entity and needs to be queryable by the auth pipeline before tenant context is set
